bead.title: checks: wire preclaim-intake and recovery hooks into the production reachability graph
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The deadcode RTA artifact reports the pre-claim intake hook and the post-ladder-exhaustion recovery hooks as unreachable, even though they are part of the live worker/recovery path. ddx-83440482 cannot close until these are genuinely reachable from a production entry point or removed.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json reports dead: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:368 — NewPreClaimIntakeHook (constructed from workers.go but the constructing path is itself unreachable in the RTA graph) and decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult; cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:107 — NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook; cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go:40 — NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook. A prior attempt (no_changes) recommended this be split into a dedicated 'wire production infrastructure into the reachability graph' bead.

PROPOSED FIX
WIRE these hooks so the production graph reaches them: ensure the constructing call site (workers.go / the recovery manager) is itself reachable from a binary entry point, and that NewPreClaimIntakeHook, NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook, and NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook are invoked on that live path. Where a symbol is a pure test wrapper with no production role (e.g. decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult), DELETE it and fold its assertions into the caller test. For any symbol needing deeper investigation beyond ~15 minutes, annotate `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file the narrower follow-up before closing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not touch the ratelimit_retry cluster (sibling bead).
- Do not touch profile_select / service_run / triage_dispatch / repair_prompt disposition (sibling bead).
- Do not touch candidate-cycle, compare, or execute-bead residuals.
- Do not change the production-reachability protocol or checker implementation.
bead.acceptance:
1. Each symbol listed for this cluster (NewPreClaimIntakeHook, decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult, NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook, NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook) in .ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json is either reachable from a production entry point or deleted.
2. `cd cli && go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./... | rg 'internal/agent/(preclaim_intake_hook|recovery_decompose|recovery_reframe)\.go'` returns no hits.
3. Behavior of the wired hooks is asserted green: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run 'TestPreClaimIntakeHook_DispatchesWithStandardProfileNoStrongPowerTrick|TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge|TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersReframe'` passes.
4. Any remaining `// wiring:pending` annotation created while resolving this cluster cites an open follow-up bead.
5. Decisions log written to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/decisions.md records WIRE|DELETE|PENDING for each listed symbol.
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
